original_text: Strategic initiatives emphasize heavy investment in research and development to maintain technological leadership, with R&D intensity at 16.31% of revenue in Q4 2025. The company's financial position remains robust, with a current ratio of 2.01 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.17, providing flexibility for continued innovation and potential strategic acquisitions. Free cash flow margin of 21.56% in Q4 2025 demonstrates strong cash generation capability. Stock performance has significantly outpaced benchmarks, with a 1-year excess return of 53.47% versus the S&P 500, reflecting investor confidence in management's execution of the AI and cloud growth strategy under CEO Sundar Pichai's leadership.
revised_text: Alphabet Inc. maintains R&D intensity at 16.31% of revenue in Q4 2025. The company's financial position shows a current ratio of 2.01 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.17. Free cash flow margin of 21.56% in Q4 2025 demonstrates strong cash generation capability. Stock performance has outpaced benchmarks, with a 1-year excess return of 53.47% versus the S&P 500. The company operates under CEO Sundar Pichai's leadership.
changes_summary: Removed unsupported claim about strategic initiatives emphasizing R&D investment (Issue 1 - C001 only contains company profile, not strategy). Removed interpretive language about financial ratios providing flexibility for innovation and acquisitions (Issue 2 - ratios don't support strategic implications). Removed causal attribution linking stock performance to investor confidence in AI/cloud strategy execution (Issue 3 - K017 only calculates excess return, doesn't explain drivers). Retained all factual metrics with proper citations and CEO information from C001.
